explanation: Using the -EncodedCommand parameter allows the execution of a Base64 encoded payload (which decodes to 'Add-PSSnapin Microsoft.Exchange.Management.PowerShell.SnapIn'). Because the Sigma rule inspects the CommandLine field strictly for plaintext strings, the encoded payload completely bypasses both string-matching selections.

The rule uses 'all of selection_*' logic, requiring the exact literal token 'Add-PSSnapin' to be found on the command line. By breaking up the cmdlet string using string concatenation and invoking it dynamically via the call operator (&), the static string pattern in 'selection_cli' is broken while achieving the same functional execution.

The detection rule enforces an explicit string match on the Exchange snap-in assembly names (e.g., 'Microsoft.Exchange.Management.PowerShell.SnapIn') within the process execution arguments. Obfuscating or concatenating the snap-in name string inside the PowerShell script breaks the static 'selection_module' filter matching logic.
